title: At the Bus Stop
summary: A man and a woman meet at a bus stop and have a short conversation about which bus to take and how long they need to wait.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Excuse me, is this the bus stop for the number seven bus?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Yes, it is. The number seven goes to the city centre.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Great! Do you know how long the wait is?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: About ten minutes, I think. I checked the timetable just now.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Thank you. I need to get to the train station.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: The number seven stops right outside the train station.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: That is very helpful. Is it a long ride?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: About fifteen minutes on the bus, depending on the traffic.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Do you know how much the fare is?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: I think it is two dollars fifty. You can pay with a card or cash.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: I only have cash today. That is fine.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Oh, here comes the bus now!

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Perfect timing! Thank you so much for your help.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: No problem. I hope you catch your train!

annotations.exampleEn: We waited at the bus stop for twenty minutes.
The museum is in the city centre, near the main square.
Do you know how long the wait is for the next bus?

annotations.meaningVi: lịch trình, thời gian biểu (của xe buýt/tàu)
annotations.exampleEn: She checked the bus timetable on her phone.
The train station is a ten-minute walk from here.
The bus stops right outside the school gate.
It is a short ride — only about five minutes.

annotations.meaningVi: tùy thuộc vào tình trạng giao thông
annotations.exampleEn: The journey takes about thirty minutes, depending on the traffic.
The bus fare is two dollars for adults.
Do you pay by card or cash?
The bus arrived just as we reached the stop — perfect timing!

grammarNotes.title: Indirect questions: Do you know...?
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi hỏi thông tin một cách lịch sự, người ta thường dùng 'Do you know...?' trước câu hỏi gián tiếp. Lưu ý thứ tự từ trong câu hỏi gián tiếp KHÔNG đảo ngữ: 'Do you know how long the wait is?' (không phải '...how long is the wait?'). 'Do you know how much the fare is?' (không phải '...how much is the fare?'). Cấu trúc: Do you know + từ hỏi + chủ ngữ + động từ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When asking for information politely, English speakers use 'Do you know...?' before an indirect question. Notice that the word order in the indirect question is NOT inverted: 'Do you know how long the wait is?' (NOT '...how long is the wait?'). Structure: Do you know + question word + subject + verb.

grammarNotes.title: Can – Expressing ability or possibility
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong bài này, 'can' được dùng để nói về khả năng hoặc sự cho phép: 'You can pay with a card or cash.' Nghĩa là 'Bạn được phép / Bạn có thể trả bằng thẻ hoặc tiền mặt.' 'Can' luôn đi kèm với động từ nguyên thể (không chia): 'can pay', 'can go', 'can see'. Không nói 'can pays' hay 'can going'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: In this passage, 'can' expresses ability or permission: 'You can pay with a card or cash.' This means it is possible and allowed. 'Can' is always followed by the base form of the verb (no changes): 'can pay', 'can go', 'can see'. Never say 'can pays' or 'can going'.

translationVi:
Người đàn ông: Xin lỗi, đây có phải là trạm xe buýt số bảy không?
Người phụ nữ: Vâng, đúng vậy. Xe số bảy đi đến trung tâm thành phố.
Người đàn ông: Tuyệt! Bạn có biết phải chờ bao lâu không?
Người phụ nữ: Khoảng mười phút, tôi nghĩ vậy. Tôi vừa xem lịch trình xong.
Người đàn ông: Cảm ơn. Tôi cần đến ga tàu hỏa.
Người phụ nữ: Xe số bảy dừng ngay bên ngoài ga tàu hỏa.
Người đàn ông: Thật hữu ích. Chuyến đi có dài không?
Người phụ nữ: Khoảng mười lăm phút trên xe buýt, tùy thuộc vào tình trạng giao thông.
Người đàn ông: Bạn có biết giá vé bao nhiêu không?
Người phụ nữ: Tôi nghĩ là hai đô rưỡi. Bạn có thể trả bằng thẻ hoặc tiền mặt.
Người đàn ông: Hôm nay tôi chỉ có tiền mặt thôi. Không sao cả.
Người phụ nữ: Ồ, xe buýt đến rồi!
Người đàn ông: Đúng lúc quá! Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều đã giúp đỡ.
Người phụ nữ: Không có gì. Hy vọng bạn bắt kịp chuyến tàu!

questions.prompt: Which bus is the man looking for?
questions.explanation: The man asks 'is this the bus stop for the number seven bus?'
questions.hint: What bus number does the man ask about?

questions.prompt: Where does the number seven bus go?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'The number seven goes to the city centre.'
questions.hint: What destination does the woman name?

questions.prompt: How long does the woman think the wait is?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'About ten minutes, I think.'
questions.hint: What time estimate does the woman give?

questions.prompt: How did the woman know the wait time?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'I checked the timetable just now.'
questions.hint: What did the woman look at to find the information?

questions.prompt: Where does the man need to go?
questions.explanation: The man says 'I need to get to the train station.'
questions.hint: What is the man's final destination?

questions.prompt: Where does the number seven bus stop relative to the train station?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'The number seven stops right outside the train station.'
questions.hint: How convenient is the bus stop for the train station?

questions.prompt: How long is the bus ride, approximately?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'About fifteen minutes on the bus.'
questions.hint: What journey time does the woman give?

questions.prompt: What can affect the length of the bus ride?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'depending on the traffic.'
questions.hint: What outside factor can change the journey time?

questions.prompt: How much does the woman think the fare is?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'I think it is two dollars fifty.'
questions.hint: What price does the woman mention for the bus fare?

questions.prompt: How does the man pay today?
questions.explanation: The man says 'I only have cash today.'
questions.hint: What payment method does the man say he has?

questions.prompt: What does the woman wish the man at the end?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'I hope you catch your train!'
questions.hint: What transport-related wish does the woman give?

cloze.template: Man: Excuse me, is this the bus stop for the number {{b1}} bus? Woman: Yes, it is. The number seven goes to the city {{b2}}. Man: Great! Do you know how long the {{b3}} is? Woman: About ten minutes, I think. I checked the {{b4}} just now. Man: Thank you. I need to get to the train {{b5}}. Woman: The number seven stops right outside the train station. Man: That is very helpful. Is it a long {{b6}}? Woman: About fifteen minutes on the bus, depending on the {{b7}}. Man: Do you know how much the {{b8}} is? Woman: I think it is two dollars fifty. You can pay with a card or {{b9}}. Man: I only have cash today. That is fine. Woman: Oh, here comes the bus now! Man: {{b10}} timing! Thank you so much for your help. Woman: No problem. I hope you catch your train!

blanks.explanation: 'Number seven' — the bus route the man is looking for.
'City centre' — the main area of the city where the bus goes.
'How long the wait is' — asking about time before the bus arrives.
'The timetable' — a printed or digital schedule showing bus times.
'Train station' — where trains depart and arrive.
'A long ride' — asking if the bus journey takes a long time.
'Depending on the traffic' — heavy traffic makes the bus slower.
'The fare' — the price you pay to travel on a bus or train.
'Card or cash' — the two payment methods the woman mentions.
'Perfect timing' — the bus arrived at exactly the right moment.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The man and woman are strangers, but they help each other at the bus stop. Why do you think it is important to be helpful to strangers in public places? Can you think of a time when a stranger helped you?
